creator: zirias@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Thomas Dettbarn from comment #10)

Yes, this looks pretty good now, thank you! Minor stylistic nitpicks left:

- Sort USES alphabetically
- Really consider PLIST_FILES instead of a pkg-plist file, at least if you don't expect the port to have considerably more files in the future

But note these are *really* nitpicks ;)

> portlint is happy.
It is, and so is portclippy and also the stage-qa :thumb:

---

So, all that's left to do is getting rid of the need to patch at all ;)
Note I added LDFLAGS=-Wl,--as-needed to the port Makefile for testing, so your upstream Makefile already allowed for custom LDFLAGS, which is nice!

---

A final tip: If you're already using git anyways, you can also do a local commit and attach the file created with 'git format-patch -1' instead of a plain diff. This will make it a lot easier to preserve author and commit-date when it is finally committed :)
